summary.intent: Implement three GitHub tasks (#297, #298, #299) adding a shared ColorValue foundation and 8 agnostic color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) to the ars-ui Rust component library, then iterate on Codex AI code review feedback until approval.
summary.outcome: All 8 components implemented with TDD, coverage CI failure fixed (89.7%→90.4% branch), PR #698 opened with 5 completed Codex review rounds (25 findings addressed across K1/K2/K3 pass 6 resolved); Codex approval (👍) not yet received at session end as round 6 verdict was still pending.

repo: core_maths::CoreFloat is the workspace's no_std float tool (used in ars-i18n via FQ form CoreFloat::method(x,..)) but lacks min/max — private fmin/fmax helpers are required
MessageFn requires manual From<F> impls per closure signature; it is not generic over all Fn types — each new signature needs a new impl block in message_fn.rs
#[derive(ComponentPart)] does not work for structs with f64 fields (f64 is not Eq/Hash) — manual impl using to_bits() is the established pattern (see input/slider Part::Tick)
Effects are NOT run by Service::send(); adapters call effect.run(ctx, props, strong_send) after draining svc.pending_effects — tests must do the same
Bindable::get() returns the controlled prop in controlled mode; a new pending() accessor returns the internally-set value needed for drag change-end callbacks
Service::new panics on empty Props.id and Service::send is &mut self — tests must inject a non-empty id and use let mut svc

CI coverage runs `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace` whose cross-crate tests boost per-crate numbers ~1.1% above local `-p` measurements; ars-components threshold is 90% line / 90% branch
cargo xci-fast has a snapshot-count gate requiring at least one snapshot per state variant — machines with 3 states need 3 distinct snapshots
CssProperty::Custom(name) renders as --{name}, so the name must NOT include the '--' prefix
Workspace uses unpinned stable toolchain; upgrading to 1.96 surfaced a pre-existing clippy::unnecessary_option_map_or_else deny in pagination/mod.rs (unrelated to new work)
spec-compile-snippets compiles spec code blocks in isolation; blocks referencing cross-block symbols need ```rust,no_check fences to avoid CI failures
Codex (chatgpt-codex-connector bot) can hit quota limits ('reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews'), silently blocking the review loop until reset

code.finding: from_hex used trim_start_matches('#') which strips ALL leading # chars, so ##3366ff was accepted as valid. Fix: strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex) strips at most one #
Non-ASCII input (e.g. #ああ) has 6+ bytes but non-ASCII chars cause byte-boundary panics at slice indexing. Fix: is_ascii() guard before any byte indexing

parse::<f64>() succeeds for 'NaN' and 'inf' strings; ColorValue::new only debug_asserts finiteness, leaking NaN into styles and ARIA in release mode. Fix: parse_finite_f64 wrapper returning None for non-finite values

Percentage channels (saturation/lightness/brightness/alpha) format as 0-100 in UI but commit_input parsed against the 0-1 range, silently clamping '50' to 1.0. Fix: divide percentage-channel input by 100 before clamping
Props::readonly only set aria-readonly, not native HtmlAttr::ReadOnly; machine still accepted Change+blur commits while readonly
Channel-mode spinbutton aria-valuenow/min/max exposed the raw 0-1 range while the visible input and commit path used 0-100 for percentage channels — ARIA values must be scaled by 100
IME CompositionStart+Change path ran before the readonly/disabled guards, allowing readonly/disabled fields to mutate input_text mid-composition
Hidden form input emitted the old hex value when ctx.invalid=true, submitting a stale color. Fix: omit the hidden input value (leave it empty) when invalid

SetToMax/drag-to-end set hue 360 which ColorValue::new wraps to 0°, making the thumb/ARIA jump to min. Fix: track unwrapped slider_value (0..=max) separately as source of truth for position and ARIA; derive color from it

Same hue-axis endpoint wrapping issue as color_slider. Fix: track x_value/y_value unwrapped in Context; chain both channels in a single read in apply_area_position to avoid second write discarding the first
SetToMax wrote hue:360 directly into ColorValue bypassing new() normalization, violating the [0,360) invariant. Fix: hue_value tracking same as slider
Increment/Decrement used % which preserves sign; large_step>360 PageDown from a small hue produced a negative hue. Fix: rem_euclid(360.0)
Wheel thumb set aria-labelledby to a *-label IDREF but ColorWheel has no Label part, creating a dangling IDREF. Fix: drop aria-labelledby (aria-label is already set)
Blur event cleared focused/focus_visible but not focused_index, leaving data-ars-focused on a stale item while the root is idle. Fix: gate focused styling on State::Focused
In idle state focused_index is None and all items get tabindex=-1, making the listbox unreachable by keyboard Tab. Fix: tabbable_index() helper gives tabindex=0 to the selected-or-first item in idle
SetProps updated min/max but left ctx.value out of the new bounds, persisting an out-of-range value in aria-valuenow and the hidden input. Fix: re-clamp value to [new_min, new_max] in SetProps arm
Bindable needed a pending() accessor returning the last internally-set value for use in controlled-mode drag change-end callbacks (get() returns the controlled prop, not the drag value)

workflow: Spec-first: always sync spec code blocks after any contract change; delegate spec mirroring to a focused subagent with exact change instructions to protect main context from large file edits
TDD for Codex findings: write failing test first, implement fix, reply to thread with test name + fix description, resolve — Codex checks resolved state before re-reviewing
DragEnd must bypass the disabled/readonly guard in all drag machines so an in-flight drag can always terminate cleanly (prevents machines stuck in Dragging state)
One-armed if-flag conditionals in *_attrs methods need explicit both-arms tests to satisfy the 90% branch coverage CI gate — a pattern that recurs across every component
`cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace` then `cargo xtask coverage check-all` is the only authoritative local check; `-p ars-components` alone undercounts by ~1%
Background watch scripts on zsh must avoid variable names like 'status' (read-only built-in); use 'cov_state' or similar
Codex review rounds converge but can require many passes (8→6→6→2→3→3) — do not merge without the explicit 👍 reaction even when CI is fully green
Mid-drag prop updates (disabled/readonly toggled) require the disabled guard to still allow DragEnd through, and DragMove to check readonly separately from the guard

friction: core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min/max despite providing most other transcendentals, requiring private fmin/fmax workarounds that diverge slightly from the established pattern

Coverage CI failed after implementation because branch coverage dropped from 92.3% to 89.7% due to one-armed conditionals in new *_attrs methods; required adding both-arms tests across all 6 stateful components
Local `-p` coverage measurement reads ~1.1% lower than CI's `--workspace` run, making it easy to think coverage is passing locally while CI will fail
TransitionPlan closure type inference (E0282) required explicit |ctx: &mut Context| annotations throughout all machine closures — not surfaced until compile time
Codex was rate-limited on the first review trigger ('reached your Codex usage limits'), blocking the loop silently for an entire session
Codex required 5+ full review passes (25+ findings) to converge; many findings were cross-cutting (same pattern in 4 drag machines), each requiring the same fix applied independently
Background watcher script crashed on zsh's read-only 'status' built-in variable, requiring a renamed variable and relaunch
`alloc::vec::Vec::new()` repeatedly triggers unnecessary_qualification lint in tests — a recurring minor friction throughout the session
Formatter reformatted files between edits, invalidating old_string matches in subsequent Edit calls and requiring re-reads

apply_area_position needed to chain both x and y channel writes in a single read — independent set_x_value/set_y_value calls each re-read the controlled color and the second discarded the first's change
open_items: Codex review round 6 reply/resolve sent and @codex review re-triggered but no 👍 verdict yet — loop must continue until approval before merge is possible
Merge of PR #698 awaits Codex 👍 + explicit user approval; issues #297/#298/#299 auto-close on merge via keywords in PR body
ColorValue spec location: currently in color-picker.md §Types; could be promoted to spec/shared/color-types.md but touches manifest + 8 component frontmatters — deferred as orthogonal cleanup
Adapter specs (leptos/dioxus-components/specialized/color-*.md) describe framework-specific adapter code explicitly out of scope for this PR — a separate future task
proptest suites are #[ignore]d (nightly-only, not in fast CI tier) — the 4 color.rs proptests and 5 specialized proptests only run in the full feature matrix CI job
spec/foundation/04-internationalization.md MessageFn From-impl listing omits several color-component signatures for consistency with existing omissions — doc-accuracy gap noted but left as-is
